headline: Sector HIGH_142 projected to exceed capacity in 18 minutes.
summary: Jarvis is synthesizing a predicted operational risk from all specialist reads: Air Marshal sees capacity pressure, Weather Boy sees severe storm intersections, Domino estimates downstream delay, Historian supplies similar bundled precedents, and Risko checks confidence drift. A westbound meter or reroute is simulation-only and still operator-approved.

agents.title: HIGH_142 capacity pressure
agents.detail: 21/18 aircraft assigned to HIGH_142; overload is 3 flights near 18:33Z.
evidence: 22 contributing flight ids drive the count

agents.title: 22 weather-route conflicts
agents.detail: Convective weather blocking eastern corridor with echo tops reaching cruise altitude.
evidence: max refc 52 dBZ; max echo top 44,000 ft

agents.title: Network impact chain
agents.detail: 43 affected flights create a 247-minute delay proxy if the peak is left alone.
evidence: arrival banks at ORD and EWR inherit delay

agents.title: 8 precedent windows matched
agents.detail: Mock historical retrieval found a close HIGH_142 pattern. Western reroute observed success rate 83% across matched precedents.
evidence: 2025-08-13 18:15Z: 91% similar, rerouted a narrow set of weather-conflicted flights.
2025-09-04 21:00Z: 84% similar, metered inbound flow before peak demand.
2026-03-19 15:30Z: 78% similar, held marginal departures until the sector recovered.

recommendations.title: Meter or Reroute Westbound Flow
recommendations.summary: Meter westbound flow before the HIGH_142 peak and route via the western corridor.
recommendations.expected_impact: Relieves HIGH_142 to ~96% capacity; ~21% delay reduction.
risks: Shifts 12% capacity burden to adjacent sector HIGH_144.

support: Risko support: Air Marshal and Weather Boy agree on cause
Risko support: 8 historical precedents favor western reroute in the mock record
weaknesses: Risko caveat: storm propagation exceeds precedent by 38%
Risko caveat: delay estimate is an inspectable heuristic, not a certified prediction

divergence_alarm.reason: Risko flags storm propagation exceeding historical match by 38%. Historical recommendation confidence is reduced from 91% to 87%.
